Adoption remains anchored by 12.4% YoY revenue growth and 72.4 adoption percentile, but that cannot offset the institutional capitulation and negative price momentum (31.5 percentile).
narratives.why_changed: No prior-day data provided; today's read reflects a portfolio in active institutional unwind. The 13F signal is distributing at -0.7 conviction (weighted -0.855), with Franklin Resources, State Street, and BlackRock collectively holding 34% of tracked shares. Insider activity on 2026-05-19 shows CEO Tyler Brian S., CFO Britt J. Vitalone, and Officer LeAnn B. Smith executing open-market (non-10b5-1) sales totaling $12.0M—a high-information sell signal absent any counterbalancing buy cluster.
narratives.why_not_to_sell: Adoption Momentum (72.4) and revenue growth (12.4% YoY) remain genuine and percentile-strong within the Health Care cohort, anchoring the case for a hold rather than a capitulation exit. Financial Evolution is constructive on gross margins (50.2 subscore) despite OCF margin compression (-1.1% TTM), suggesting the core business is not yet broken. The binding constraint is pure capital flows: institutional distribution and insider sales are liquidity-driven, not thesis-breaking, and a rebalance into the stock would materially improve the institutional confidence pillar without requiring operational change.
narratives.what_would_break: A drop below 40 (Structural Review lower bound) would trigger a formal thesis review and likely trigger re-rating into a reduce-on-strength candidate; that would require either a revision to adoption momentum below 50 (implying revenue deceleration below 5% YoY) or further institutional capitulation (net sellers exceeding 90% of the cohort). Conversely, cluster buying (3+ insiders purchasing in 30 days) or a stabilization in 13F conviction signal to mixed or accumulating would lift Institutional Confidence above 50 and move the composite into Monitor (60–69 band), materially improving conviction.
